frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing 'LOOPS AND WIRE' by Masie Rahimi MD from Amsterdam UMC, ASC logo visible. No task activity yet.
FLS trainer box visible with Maryland dissector instrument visible. Two green wires (alligator clip strings) securing lower corners of gauze. No gauze marked circle yet visible in frame.
Maryland dissector grasping gauze with jumbo clip visible on gauze. Green wires taut, securing traction. Equipment positioned correctly.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good traction established; clip placement adequate

frame_analyses.description: Scissors now visible in field alongside Maryland dissector. Gauze positioned centrally. Jumbo clip holds gauze layers. Setup appears complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both instruments now in view; ready for entry cut

frame_analyses.description: Scissors positioned at edge of gauze, beginning initial entry cut from periphery toward marked circle. Dissector provides counter-traction. Cut appears to enter from gauze edge.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry technique correct - cutting from edge as required

frame_analyses.description: Scissors advancing through gauze with Maryland dissector elevating tissue to maintain visibility. Cut progressing from edge. Jumbo clip maintaining position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth entry cut progression, good instrument coordination

frame_analyses.description: Cut now near the marked circular boundary. Scissors tracking toward circle. Dissector continues counter-traction. Circle outline becoming visible in relation to cut path.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Approaching circle; transitioning to circle-following phase

frame_analyses.description: Scissors now actively following marked circle perimeter. Cut proceeding along line. Maryland dissector maintaining elevation. Approximately 1/4 to 1/3 of circle cut so far.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Circle-following initiated; line adherence appears good

frame_analyses.description: Scissors continuing around circle. Approximately 50% of circle now cut free from surrounding gauze. Cut path appears to track the marked line. Dissector managing tissue.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady circumferential progress; mid-circle point reached

frame_analyses.description: Approximately 70-80% of circle cut. Scissors continuing smooth tracing. One section of circle (bottom right quadrant) still attached. Cut quality appears consistent.
Final section of marked circle being cut. Scissors making terminal cuts. Circle is becoming fully detached from surrounding gauze matrix. Dissector stabilizing remaining attachment point.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final release phase; precise terminal cuts visible

frame_analyses.description: Marked circle now completely freed from gauze. Both instrument tips visible; circle segment fully detached and held in jumbo clip. Task appears complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean separation achieved; circle fully released

penalties.description: Video is instructional/demonstration content with narration overlay and title slide. Actual cut path visibility is limited by camera angle and the demonstration nature of the content. Conservative assessment: no measurable deviation observable from available frames, but full-circle cardinal-point assessment cannot be performed due to video classification.

cutting_accuracy.notes: Instructional video format limits precise deviation measurement. Cut path appears to track marked line throughout visible phases, but oblique camera angles and demonstration context prevent quantitative deviation scoring.

score_components.score_caveat: This is an INSTRUCTIONAL VIDEO. Official FLS scoring does not apply. Estimated score provided for reference only.

confidence_rationale: Video is classified as instructional/demonstration content with title slide, narration context, and educational framing. This is NOT a scored performance attempt. Completion time can be estimated (~62 seconds, expert-level speed), but penalty assessment for line deviation is unreliable due to oblique camera angles and video production/editing context. Confidence is low because: (1) instructional videos often use edited footage, (2) camera positioning varies throughout, (3) marked circle line contrast may be adjusted for teaching clarity, (4) this is demonstration, not live performance.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation from marked circle line at cardinal points — camera angle is oblique and inconsistent across phases
Whether this is a single continuous performance or edited instructional montage
Actual execution time (video may contain cuts or slow-motion for teaching purposes)
Official penalty score — instructional content is not scored under FLS rubric
video_classification_rationale: Title slide explicitly identifies this as 'LOOPS AND WIRE' instructional content by Masie Rahimi MD from Amsterdam UMC. The presence of an institutional logo, narration context, and structured educational framing (title-to-task format) classifies this as INSTRUCTIONAL material, not a trainee performance or expert demo. The video appears to be an educational tutorial on FLS skills.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper gauze traction setup with alligator clips and jumbo clip
Entry cut initiated from gauze edge toward marked circle (not direct circle approach)
Circumferential scissor technique for circular cutting
Maryland dissector use for counter-traction and tissue elevation during cutting
Scissors positioned tangentially to curve for smooth circle following
Terminal cut technique for final circle release
verbal_cues: Title indicates task: 'LOOPS AND WIRE' by instructor Masie Rahimi MD
common_errors_addressed: Not entering from edge (instructional approach demonstrates proper entry point)
Loss of visual contact with marked line (video shows continuous line visualization)
Ungainly instrument handoffs (smooth dissector-scissors coordination demonstrated)

improvement_suggestions: Note: This is instructional content, not a performance score. Provided only for completeness.
summary: This is an instructional/educational video on FLS Pattern Cut task from Amsterdam UMC. It is NOT a scored performance attempt. The video demonstrates proper technique including edge entry, smooth circle following, and expert-level cutting speed (~62s). Teaching content value is high; official FLS scoring does not apply. Confidence in estimated metrics is low due to instructional context and edited nature of demonstration footage.
